title: Purged Rolling-Origin Validation By Race Session
summary: The newest F1 tyre-degradation paper evaluates one-step-ahead predictions with rolling-origin cross-validation inside sessions, which matches the causal structure of lap-by-lap strategy better than shuffled folds.
rationale: The newest F1 tyre-degradation paper evaluates one-step-ahead predictions with rolling-origin cross-validation inside sessions, which matches the causal structure of lap-by-lap strategy better than shuffled folds. CatBoost docs also note default CV shuffles unless grouping is provided. For this target, leakage from nearby laps in the same stint is a real selection trap.
implementation_hint: Select models with outer folds grouped by `Year,Race`, and inside each training fold evaluate with forward-only splits ordered by lap within `Driver,Race,Stint`. Recompute any target/statistical encodings strictly inside each fold. Compare whether current 0.951 CV models remain stable under this harsher protocol before trusting LB deltas.
expected_effect: May lower local CV but improve model selection and reduce public-LB disappointment from within-race leakage.
risk: If the current pipeline is already robust, this mostly changes selection variance rather than raw model strength.
